content.question: Is the current surplus of dormitory places available for 6-/8-year track applicants (age 10-12), and are existing welfare, supervision, and pastoral-care models adequate for pre-adolescent boarders, or do they require age-specific modification? What is the marginal cost of scaling kollégium provision if demand were to increase?
content.reduction_path: Conduct an audit of kollégium occupancy disaggregated by track type and student age-group, survey kollégium staff on perceived adequacy of duty ratios and pastoral models for 10-12-year-olds, and obtain marginal cost estimates from KLIK for expanding or reconfiguring dormitory capacity for younger cohorts.
